standing: SECONDARY - candidate generator only, never a fact witness
NOT_A_FACT_SOURCE: Nothing in this file is a fact about the CoA client. These are CANDIDATES - questions for the live probe to adjudicate. Do not cite reference/ as basis/. The wiki's own Resources section points at Wowpedia and wowwiki-archive for its conditional reference, i.e. it inherits from RETAIL docs rather than verifying against this client - exactly as unverified as agent recall.
why_allowed: Recall and secondary sources are inadmissible as a FACT source but admissible as a QUESTION source, because the probe adjudicates. A wrong candidate costs one probe row reading 'unsupported'. A wrong fact poisons the basis silently.
absence_proves_nothing: This is a beginner guide, not a reference. A conditional it omits is UN-ASKED, not unsupported. The rim stays open after probing this list.

@mouseover.wiki_says: Casts on the unit your mouse is hovering over

@party1.wiki_says: Casts on specific party members (group order)

@party4.wiki_says: Casts on specific party members (group order)

exists.wiki_says: The unit exists (i.e., target is valid)

group.wiki_says: You are in a party or raid

stance:X.wiki_says: You are in a specific stance/form (e.g., Shadowform)

grammar_claims: clauses evaluate in order; the FIRST true one wins
#showtooltip as first line drives dynamic tooltip

two_mechanisms.why_this_matters: `@unit` and `[flag]` are NOT the same mechanism and do NOT have the same proof method. Conflating them is the easiest way to write a confident wrong guide.

@unit / target=unit.mechanism: HYPOTHESIS: a pass-through STRING. SecureCmdOptionParse appears to return whatever follows @, and support lives DOWNSTREAM - in the handler's own special-casing (/cast checks target=='cursor' and hands off to Custom_HandleTerrainClick) and in unit resolution.
@unit / target=unit.consequence: the differential-polarity matrix does NOT apply - there is likely no [no@cursor]. Asking 'is @cursor supported' is really asking two questions: does the parser return it, and does anything downstream honour it.
@unit / target=unit.probe_row_that_settles_it: SecureCmdOptionParse("[@banana] X") - if it returns target="banana" for a nonsense unit, @ is pass-through and the vocabulary question does not apply to it at all.
@unit / target=unit.status: HYPOTHESIS - not asserted. The probe settles it in one row.

[flag].mechanism: actually EVALUATED by the C parser (combat, mod:shift, group, ...).
[flag].consequence: this is what the differential-polarity matrix in basis/conditionals.json is for. THIS is the vocabulary question.

corroborations.what_it_proves: the client's own Lua compares the parser's returned target to "cursor", so a HANDLER anticipates @cursor and SecureCmdOptionParse can return it. This proves the HANDLER supports it - it does NOT prove the parser validates it (see two_mechanisms).
corroborations.standing: SOURCE-CORROBORATED (handler-side). Still wants the live probe.

corroboration_note: The ONLY source witness the conditional vocabulary has on this client. It reaches TARGET TOKENS only (a handler comparing the parser's returned target to a literal), never flags like [combat]. Everything else in `candidates` has NO source witness of any kind and is purely a question for the probe.
